mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 100,000 jobs signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2010 payroll levels at 132,266k suggest post-crisis recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remaining below 2.5% signals an accommodative monetary stance that reduces equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of June 2010 indicates continued accommodation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 1,500k units signals weakening residential demand and reduced construction employment multiplier effects, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations contract.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1500 (thousands of units) in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,771k in May 2010 remains elevated but vulnerable to demand shocks
regime_hint: post-crisis housing market recovery with fragile demand
failure_condition: Housing starts decline below 1,500k but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and reduced corporate capital expenditure, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as near-term earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth rate falls below 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.813 in June 2010 reflects post-recession recovery
failure_condition: Industrial production deceleration below 0.2% MoM does not precede negative 5-day returns in at least 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining unemployment rate signals strengthening consumer demand and labor market tightness. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through accelerating corporate revenue growth and margin expansion.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.3% MoM AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp in prior month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 349,436M and UNRATE at 5.4% in June 2010 show mixed signals
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand and labor tightening
failure_condition: Joint signal of rising retail sales and falling unemployment does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 58% of triggered instances, or interaction effect (coefficient on RSAFS × UNRATE term) is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, creating negative real rates that erode equity risk premiums and signal monetary policy lag. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the Fed eventually tightens.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag regime with rising inflation and accommodative rates
failure_condition: Negative real rate signal does not precede negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) does not have statistically significant predictive power (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.5% in a single month, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline due to weakening demand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% in June 2010 is near the trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase in post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Unemployment rate spike above 5.5% does not precede negative 20-day returns in at least 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized in the most recent quarter, signaling weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10,891.0B (Q1 2010) reflects post-recession recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Real GDP deceleration below 2.0% annualized does not precede negative 60-day returns in at least 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
